description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for Chapter 6: Measuring Length

excellent_performance.rationale: Student has excellent understanding of measurement basics and ruler use, can fast-track to conversions and word problems

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to units of length: cm, m, km

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready, provide complete coverage of all measurement topics

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to units of length: cm, m, km
Converting cm to m and m to cm
Converting m to km and km to m

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps in measurement basics or ruler use, needs targeted review before length concepts

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

exampleSequence.reason: Review: What is measurement and why we measure
Review: How to use a ruler correctly
Review: Addition and subtraction for measurement problems
Start main topic: Units of length cm, m, km

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant prerequisite review in measurement basics and ruler use before attempting length conversions

videoSequence.reason: Essential: Learn to use a ruler correctly
Critical: Review addition and subtraction for length calculations
Gentle intro to units of length
Begin main content: Using ruler and measuring tape

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these prerequisite videos, retake the readiness quiz to check if you are ready to continue with measuring length.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

measurement_concept.conceptName: Understanding what measurement means and why we measure

using_ruler.conceptName: Using a ruler to measure objects in centimetres

addition_subtraction.conceptName: Adding and subtracting numbers up to 1000

comparing_lengths.conceptName: Comparing lengths of two objects (longer, shorter, same)

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: How many centimetres are there in 1 metre?
questions.explanation: 1 metre = 100 centimetres. This is a very important fact for measuring length!

questions.question: Neha measures a book and it is 25 cm long. What unit did she use?
questions.explanation: She used centimetres (cm). Centimetres are used to measure small objects like books and pencils.

ifAllCorrect.message: Excellent! You understand measurement basics well.

ifAllWrong.reason: Student struggling with basic measurement, review ruler use

questions.question: How many metres are there in 1 kilometre?
questions.explanation: 1 kilometre = 1000 metres. Kilometres are used to measure long distances like roads between cities.

ifCorrect.reason: Student ready for length word problems

ifWrong.message: Watch the metres to kilometres video again, then try some practice problems.

skip_ahead.action: Skip estimation video, jump to word problems or next topic

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review on measurement basics

suggest_break.trigger: After 4 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_practice.trigger: Student completes videos but scores < 70% on checkpoint
recommend_practice.action: Recommend practising measurement with a real ruler and objects at home

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before main content

high_score.message: Excellent! You know your measurement basics really well. You are ready to learn about measuring length in different units. We have created a fast-track playlist with challenge content for you.

good_score.message: Good work! You understand measurement and rulers well. Let's learn about centimetres, metres, and kilometres with complete coverage of all the topics.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in measurement basics or ruler use. Don't worry! We will start with targeted review videos on the concepts you need, then move to measuring length.

low_score.message: You need to review measurement basics and ruler use first. We have created a playlist focused on building a strong foundation. After watching, you can retake the quiz and then learn about measuring length.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how length measurement concepts build on each other

concepts: Units of length: cm, m, km

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 70% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

conversionFluency.description: % of students who can correctly convert between cm, m, and km

estimationAccuracy.description: % of students who can estimate length within 20% of actual measurement

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 40-50 minutes with 90-100% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 35-40 minutes with 80-90% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 50-55 minutes with 70-80% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (25 min) + retake + main content (25 min) = 50-60 total minutes, expected 60-75% on practice quiz

commonChallenges.challenge1: Students confusing cm and m conversions (forgetting whether to multiply or divide by 100)
commonChallenges.solution1: Use the mnemonic: 'metre is the BIG unit, so the number gets SMALLER when converting cm to m. Divide by 100.'
commonChallenges.challenge2: Students unable to estimate lengths realistically
commonChallenges.solution2: Teach body-based benchmarks: fingernail width = 1 cm, arm span = about 1 m, 10 minutes of walking = about 1 km.
commonChallenges.challenge3: Students who can convert but struggle with word problems
commonChallenges.solution3: Teach the CUBES strategy: Circle numbers, Underline the question, Box key words, Evaluate the operation, Solve and check.
